metadata.working_title: Mapping responses to focal injections of bicuculline along the rostrocaudal axis of the parafacial respiratory region in rats

abstract_sketch.problem: Active expiration is generated by a population of ventral medullary neurons in the parafacial lateral (pFL) region, but the precise rostrocaudal organization of the expiratory oscillator relative to the caudal tip of the facial nucleus (VIIc) remains unresolved.
abstract_sketch.gap: Previous studies have used variable coordinates (ranging from -0.3 to +1.0 mm from VIIc) to target pFL, yielding mixed characterizations of respiratory and abdominal muscle responses. A systematic within-study comparison of GABAergic disinhibition at multiple rostrocaudal coordinates in the same preparation has been missing.
abstract_sketch.approach: We bilaterally pressure-injected the GABA-A receptor antagonist bicuculline methochloride (200 microM, 200 nL per side) into one of five rostrocaudal coordinates (-0.2, +0.1, +0.4, +0.6, +0.8 mm from VIIc; n=5-7 per group) or HEPES vehicle (CTRL, n=7) in anesthetized, vagotomized adult rats and recorded abdominal (ABD) and diaphragm (DIA) EMG, airflow, tidal volume, minute ventilation, and oxygen consumption for 20-25 min post-injection. Injection sites were verified with fluorobeads and cFos/PHOX2B immunohistochemistry. Respiratory cycles were analyzed both as univariate time series and as 3D trajectories combining airflow, integrated DIA, and integrated ABD signals.
abstract_sketch.key_results: Bicuculline elicited active expiration at every tested coordinate, but the +0.6 and +0.8 mm locations produced the most robust and prolonged responses. ABD response duration was longest at +0.6 mm (17.6 +/- 2.7 min) and +0.8 mm (17.1 +/- 3.3 min) compared with the most caudal site (-0.2 mm = 2.4 +/- 1.1 min). Response onset was fastest at +0.6 mm (88.7 +/- 32.3 s before the second injection). Peak tidal volume was highest at +0.6 mm (10.8 +/- 0.5 ml/kg, a 29% increase from baseline), minute ventilation rose 16% at +0.6 mm but fell 12% at -0.2 mm, and oxygen consumption dropped by 33% at +0.6 mm and 25% at +0.8 mm. 3D trajectory analysis further dissociated the two rostral sites: +0.6 mm produced the largest inspiratory "bulbs", while +0.8 mm produced the longest late-expiratory "tails" and largest post-inspiratory "feet".
abstract_sketch.impact: The findings provide a high-resolution functional map of the pFL, identify +0.6 to +0.8 mm rostral to VIIc as the core of the expiratory oscillator, and demonstrate that small (<=400 micrometer) shifts along the rostrocaudal axis qualitatively change the pattern of active expiration, with implications for studies of respiratory control, cardiovascular coupling, and disorders of breathing.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Breathing is generated by distributed neural oscillators in the ventral medulla; inspiration by the preBotzinger complex (preBotC) and active expiration by the parafacial respiratory region.
The parafacial lateral (pFL) subregion is a candidate conditional expiratory oscillator that recruits abdominal muscles during high metabolic demand.
GABAergic and glycinergic disinhibition of pFL with bicuculline (or bicuculline/strychnine) reliably evokes active expiration in anesthetized rodents.

Published studies have targeted pFL at coordinates ranging from -0.3 to +1.0 mm from the caudal tip of the facial nucleus (VIIc), producing qualitatively different ABD response patterns (tonic, late-expiratory, post-inspiratory) and leaving the anatomical core of pFL debated.

We systematically compare bicuculline responses at five rostrocaudal coordinates in the same preparation, combining EMG, plethysmography, metabolic measurements, multivariate 3D trajectory analysis, and cFos/PHOX2B histology.

We show that pFL is functionally continuous along the rostrocaudal axis but that small shifts (<=400 micrometer) qualitatively reorganize the active-expiratory pattern, with +0.6 to +0.8 mm from VIIc producing the most robust responses.

subsections.title: Histological verification of injection sites and cFos activation
content_bullets: n=28 bicuculline and n=7 CTRL rats, 5 rostrocaudal groups (-0.2, +0.1, +0.4, +0.6, +0.8 mm from VIIc)
CTRL: 44.7 +/- 4.0 cFos+ cells/hemisection along rostrocaudal axis; 2.3 +/- 1.0 cFos+/PHOX2B+ cells
Bicuculline: peak activation 104.5 +/- 5.1 cFos+ cells at core of injection (-0.2 mm=89.7; +0.1=123.1; +0.4=110.2; +0.6=98.3; +0.8=101.2); 44.8 +/- 1.2 cells beyond injection boundary
cFos+/PHOX2B+ counts unchanged from CTRL (2.4 +/- 0.4 vs 2.3 +/- 1.0), indicating minimal RTN engagement

subsections.title: Active expiration is recruited at every rostrocaudal coordinate but is more robust and enduring rostrally
content_bullets: ABD response elicited in 3/5 rats at -0.2 mm and in all rats at +0.1, +0.4, +0.6, +0.8 mm
Tonic ABD preceding late-E peak only in +0.6 and +0.8 mm groups
Post-I ABD peak in 5/6 rats at +0.6 mm only
ABD response duration: -0.2 mm = 2.4 +/- 1.1 min vs +0.6 = 17.6 +/- 2.7 min, +0.8 = 17.1 +/- 3.3 min (ANOVA p=0.043, eta2=0.41)
Onset latency: +0.6 mm response began 88.7 +/- 32.3 s before the second injection; other groups 20-40 s after second injection (ANOVA p=0.041, eta2=0.57)

subsections.title: Respiratory effects scale with rostrocaudal coordinate
content_bullets: Respiratory frequency dropped in all groups but significantly only at -0.2 mm and +0.1 mm (RM-ANOVA p=0.003, eta2=0.46)
Nadir of frequency reached at 2 min (-0.2 mm), 4 min (+0.1, +0.4 mm), 6 min (+0.6, +0.8 mm), confirming a rostrocaudal timing gradient

Inspiratory duration decreased (Ti response=0.279 +/- 0.034 s vs baseline 0.318 +/- 0.043 s; Wilcoxon Z=3.24, p=0.001); expiratory duration increased (TE response=1.313 +/- 0.188 s vs baseline 1.029 +/- 0.161 s; Z=4.49, p=0.001)
Peak VT at +0.6 mm = 10.8 +/- 0.5 ml/kg (29% increase) vs -0.2 mm = 7.0 +/- 0.4 ml/kg (8%) and +0.1 mm = 8.0 +/- 0.3 ml/kg (16%); RM-ANOVA p=0.01, eta2=0.44
VE at -0.2 mm decreased 12% (255.4 +/- 16.2 ml/min/kg); +0.6 mm increased 16% (414.3 +/- 22.7); RM-ANOVA p=0.001, eta2=0.59
VO2 fell only at rostral sites: +0.6 = 11.8 +/- 0.8 ml/min/kg (33% drop); +0.8 = 12.2 +/- 6.1 ml/min/kg (25% drop); RM-ANOVA p=0.0001, eta2=0.59

subsections.title: Time-resolved effects on airflow and ABD EMG
content_bullets: Expiratory airflow: +0.6 mm vs CTRL significant from 4 min (KW H(5)=14.66, p=0.012) to 12 min (KW H(5)=18.35, p=0.003)
ABD EMG: +0.6 and +0.8 mm vs -0.2 mm and CTRL from 8 to 14 min (KW H(5)=20.81, p<0.001); +0.8 mm elevated at 16 min (KW H(4)=13.56, p=0.009)
Inspiratory airflow at +0.6 mm vs CTRL significant from 2 min (KW H(4)=13.25, p=0.021) to 12 min (KW H(4)=14.58, p=0.012); +0.6 vs +0.4 from 10-12 min
+0.8 mm post-I integral ABD elevated vs -0.2 mm at 6 min (KW H(4)=10.37, p=0.035) and 16 min (KW H(4)=9.52, p=0.049)

subsections.title: 3D trajectory analysis dissociates +0.6 mm and +0.8 mm responses
content_bullets: Airflow x integrated DIA x integrated ABD trajectories partition the respiratory cycle into late-E tails, inspiratory bulbs, post-I feet (Figure 6)
+0.6 mm tails > -0.2 mm from 8 min (KW H(4)=12.22, p=0.016) to 12 min (KW H(4)=12.34, p=0.015); +0.8 mm tails > -0.2 mm from 12 to 16 min
+0.6 mm inspiratory bulbs > -0.2 mm from 8 min (KW H(4)=12.59, p=0.013) to 14 min (KW H(4)=10.27, p=0.036)
+0.8 mm post-I feet > -0.2 mm from 4 min (KW H(4)=10.01, p=0.040) to 16 min (KW H(4)=9.70, p=0.046), excluding 10 and 14 min
Variability-normalized analysis confirms dissociation: +0.8 mm dominates late-E and post-I through 20 min; +0.6 mm dominates inspiration, peaking at 12 min

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: A functional map of pFL: active expiration is elicitable at every coordinate from -0.2 to +0.8 mm from VIIc, but the magnitude, onset, duration, and phase structure of the response follow a clear rostrocaudal gradient.

Identification of +0.6 to +0.8 mm from VIIc as the functional core: fastest onset, longest duration, largest VT and VE/VO2 increases, and largest 3D deformations localize the conditional expiratory oscillator rostral to VIIc in the adult rat.
Dissociation within the rostral core: +0.6 mm is primarily inspiratory-phase dominant, whereas +0.8 mm is primarily late-E and post-I dominant, consistent with a microzonal organization.

Relation to prior coordinates (Pagliardini 2011, de Britto and Moraes 2017, Huckstepp 2015, Silva 2019): our within-study comparison reconciles these reports by showing coordinate-specific response signatures.
RTN non-involvement: PHOX2B+/cFos+ counts did not change with bicuculline, indicating that the pFL expiratory effect is not a direct consequence of chemosensory RTN activation.
Mechanistic implications for respiratory-cardiovascular coupling, autonomic drive, obstructive sleep apnea, and ventilatory failure in neurological disease.
Limitations: anesthetized vagotomized preparation, adult male rats, acute pharmacological disinhibition, single timepoint histology; and future directions using cell-type-specific tools.

sections.title: Materials and Methods
content_bullets: Adult Sprague-Dawley rats; University of Alberta ACUC AUP#461
Anesthesia 5% isoflurane induction, 1-3% maintenance; tracheotomy; 30% supplemental O2; gas analyzer ML 206-1704 ADInstruments
Bilateral vagotomy (2 mm mid-cervical); rectal temperature 37 +/- 1 C with servo heating pad

Bicuculline methochloride 200 microM in HEPES, 200 nL per side at 100 nL/min via 30 micrometer glass pipette; bregma set 5 mm below lambda; lateral 2.8 mm; rostrocaudal -0.2, +0.1, +0.4, +0.6, or +0.8 mm from VIIc; CTRL = HEPES/fluorobeads

EMG electrodes in diaphragm and oblique abdominal muscles; differential amplifier model 1700 AM Systems; 10000x, 300 Hz-1 kHz band-pass; Powerlab 16/30 and 16/35 ADInstruments, 1 kHz sampling, 500 Hz anti-alias
Whole-body plethysmography with calibrated 5-point VT curve (0.5-5 ml)
Transcardial perfusion 0.9% NaCl + 4% PFA; 30 micrometer cryosections; cFos, PHOX2B, TH, NK1R immunohistochemistry; 120 micrometer serial spacing -400 to +1000 micrometer from VIIc
Data in 2-min bins, 20 min post-injection; One-way and RM ANOVA, Tukey/Bonferroni; Kruskal-Wallis + Dunn + Sidak when assumptions violated; Wilcoxon rank-sum for paired within-animal
Custom MATLAB for 3D airflow-DIA-ABD trajectory analysis, Euclidean deviation at late-E, inspiration, post-I phases
